text:
Once upon a time in a small village called Willow Creek, there was a girl named Lily. She loved birds. Every spring she would go searched far woodlands looking for feathers. She had a small pocket book. Only two feathers could fill it.

One day a blackbird came flying near. He stopped. Lily threw him feather. He sang.

You never go elsewhere. You'll sing forever.

A red owl hooted.

I think you're a fool.

I know you're here.

You come every year.

You'll go somewhere else.

I'll watch.

They say you're wild.

I come to see.

I'll never.

You'll sing forever.

I never know.

You go away.

Find another.

I stay here.

It's true. I see.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
